### [x] [engineering] [4.8] quiz — silent-linear switches under-scored by startsWith('linear') bug — addressed in edea2c0, closes #446
- category: engineering
- filed: 2026-07-11 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep)
- impact: 6 (`apps/web/src/lib/quiz/recommendSwitch.ts` used `sw.type.startsWith('linear')` in three scoring branches — crisp sound profile, smooth actuation feel, gaming primary use. `'silent-linear'` starts with `'silent-'`, not `'linear'`, so it never matched — only the bare `'linear'` type did, inconsistent with the parallel tactile handling nearby which correctly ORs `'tactile'` | `'silent-tactile'`. Hand-computed scoring for the smooth+gaming+light quiz path: a 45g silent-linear switch should score 23 (beating a 50g plain-linear switch at 19) but scored only 15 with the bug, so the plain-linear switch won instead — the live `/quiz/switch` feature silently steered "smooth"/"quiet" answers away from the objectively better silent-linear match)
- ease: 8 (three one-line boolean-check fixes, no schema/UI/route change)
- score: 4.8 (impact × ease / 10)
- action: replaced the three `sw.type.startsWith('linear')` checks in `apps/web/src/lib/quiz/recommendSwitch.ts` with explicit `(sw.type === 'linear' || sw.type === 'silent-linear')`; added regression test G asserting a lighter silent-linear switch outranks a heavier plain-linear switch under smooth+gaming+light answers
- issue: #446
> Picked as the top signal this tick: AUDIT.md's only other Pending row remains the blocked `[6.3]` march.yml crash-issue-gate item; CRITIQUE.md's only Pending row remains the non-actionable `[needs-user-call]` GA-beacon item; all mechanical surveys clean (content-gap, crosslink, companion, stale-GB, newsletter-gap, OG-coverage, a11y-spec-coverage, tracker-linkage); expand gate not due (8 commits/~5h since pass 174, threshold 20 commits/48h). A fresh general-purpose sweep targeted the switch-quiz scoring logic specifically (an angle not covered by prior passes' link/a11y/SEO/data-integrity sweeps) and found this verified, reproducible bug — sitemap.ts, robots.ts, buildRss.ts, compare selectors, MiniSearch runtime, and recommendKeycapSet.ts all checked clean. This is distinct from the still-open "unbounded lower branch in spring-weight scoring" note left by an earlier pass (line 6522 above) — that's a separate bug in `scoreSpringWeight`, not yet shipped. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, 592 unit tests, data:validate (69 records), build, 981/981 e2e.

### [x] [engineering] [4.8] quiz — medium spring-weight scoring missing lower bound — addressed in dc31421, closes #447
- category: engineering
- filed: 2026-07-11 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep)
- impact: 6 (`apps/web/src/lib/quiz/recommendSwitch.ts:47` `scoreSpringWeight`'s `'medium'` case gave the +4 "near miss" credit to any switch with actuation weight ≤65g — no lower bound — unlike the one-directional `'light'` (`g<=45?8:g<=55?4:0`) and `'heavy'` (`g>=60?8:g>=55?4:0`) cases. Any ultra-light switch nowhere near "medium" got the same near-miss credit as a switch genuinely just outside the 46-59g band on the high side. Scripting all 36 `(soundProfile × actuationFeel × primaryUse)` combos with `springWeight:'medium'` against the real switch catalog showed the top-3 recommendation list changed in 6 of 36 combos — a live-feature-affecting defect, not a corner case. This is the specific bug the prior pass's "unbounded lower branch in spring-weight scoring" note (filed alongside the sibling `startsWith('linear')` fix, `edea2c0`) had flagged but not yet shipped)
- ease: 8 (one-line fix — add a lower bound to the near-miss branch — mirroring the buffer pattern used by `light`/`heavy`; one regression test)
- score: 4.8 (impact × ease / 10)
- action: `apps/web/src/lib/quiz/recommendSwitch.ts:47` — `g >= 46 && g <= 59 ? 8 : g <= 65 ? 4 : 0` → `g >= 46 && g <= 59 ? 8 : g >= 40 && g <= 65 ? 4 : 0`. Added regression test H in `recommendSwitch.test.ts` asserting a true-medium 55g linear switch outranks an ultra-light 37g silent-linear switch under `springWeight: 'medium'` + `smooth` + `office` answers (hand-computed: 18 vs. 20 pre-fix, 18 vs. 16 post-fix)
- issue: #447
> Picked as the top signal this tick: AUDIT.md's only other Pending row remains the blocked `[6.3]` march.yml crash-issue-gate item; CRITIQUE.md's only Pending row remains the non-actionable `[needs-user-call]` GA-beacon item; all mechanical surveys clean (content-gap, crosslink, companion, stale-GB, newsletter-gap, OG-coverage, a11y-spec-coverage, tracker-linkage); expand gate not due (10 commits/~1h since pass 175, threshold 20 commits/48h). A fresh general-purpose sweep targeted compare/switch, compare/board, quiz/keycap-set, vendors, archive, and the flagged-but-unshipped spring-weight note specifically — found this verified bug and confirmed compare/switch, compare/board, /vendors, /archive, and recommendKeycapSet.ts all clean. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, 594 unit tests, data:validate (69 records), build, 981/981 e2e.

- category: engineering
- filed: 2026-07-11 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep)
- impact: 4 (`apps/web/src/app/part/[kind]/[slug]/page.tsx`'s back-link built its plural via naive `kindHuman.toLowerCase() + 's'`, rendering "← all switchs" on all 18 live `/part/switch/<slug>` pages — an irregular English plural the concatenation gets wrong. `keycap-set` → "keycap sets" and `board` → "boards" happened to pluralize correctly by coincidence, masking the bug. The sibling `/part/[kind]` listing page already carried a correct `KIND_PLURAL` map, proving the right string was known elsewhere in the codebase but never reused here — a visible, verifiable grammar error on a live editorial site that trades on precise voice)

- score: 3.6 (impact × ease / 10)

### [x] [engineering] [4.2] quiz — rapid double-click on one question silently skips the next, corrupting results — addressed in 70f2be4
- category: engineering
- filed: 2026-07-12 by cloud /iterate audit (carried over from the same-tick note above, not shipped then per one-fix-per-tick)
- impact: 6 (`SwitchQuiz.tsx`/`KeycapSetQuiz.tsx` `handleSelect` scheduled a bare `setTimeout(() => setStep(s => s + 1), 150)` on every click with no guard against re-entry. Two clicks landing on the same rendered question before the 150ms delay fires both schedule an advance, so `step` jumps by 2 — the skipped question's key stays absent from the `QuizAnswers` object fed into `recommendSwitch`/`recommendKeycapSet`, which silently scores that dimension as 0 for every candidate with no error or visible signal. Live, indexed features (`/quiz/switch`, `/quiz/keycap-set`); plausible trigger (accidental double-click, fast re-answer))
- ease: 7 (guard re-entry via a ref-backed pending timer, cleared and rescheduled on every click — same shape in both near-identical components; needed matching regression tests in both, plus fixing four e2e loops that turned out to be unknowingly relying on the race to advance through all 4 questions without waiting for each render)
- score: 4.2 (impact × ease / 10)
- action: `SwitchQuiz.tsx` and `KeycapSetQuiz.tsx` — added a `pendingAdvance` ref cleared before each new `setTimeout` schedule, so only the latest click per question survives. New regression tests in both components' test files (double-click-same-question, double-click-then-complete-quiz). Updated `apps/e2e/tests/quiz.spec.ts` (both start-over tests) and `apps/e2e/tests/parts.spec.ts` (both browse-all-parts tests) to wait for each question to render between clicks, matching the existing safe pattern already used by the "shows results" tests in the same file.
- issue: [mirror-skipped: fix already verified/committed/deployed by the time the audit concluded; best-effort per hard rule 7.7]
> Picked as the top signal this tick: no unlabeled GitHub issues (triage gate); not Monday (weekly snapshot gate skipped); AUDIT.md's only other Pending row remains the blocked `[6.3]` march.yml crash-issue-gate item (unrelated, needs `/oversight`); CRITIQUE.md's only Pending row remains the non-actionable `[needs-user-call]` GA-beacon item; all mechanical surveys clean (content-gap, crosslink, companion, stale-GB, newsletter-gap, OG-coverage, a11y-spec-coverage); no pending phases/data work; march's own expand Step 3c gate not met (10 commits/~8h30m since pass 179). Re-verified a fresh general-purpose sweep's flagged-but-unshipped secondary finding from the immediately preceding tick (the vendor heading-order fix) — re-read `SwitchQuiz.tsx`/`KeycapSetQuiz.tsx` and confirmed the double-click race was still present and unaddressed. Fixed both components plus the four e2e call sites that had been passing by accident. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, unit tests (+4 new: 2 per quiz component), data:validate, build, size (108.5 KB/200 KB), 995/995 e2e.

### [x] [engineering] [3.6] quiz pages hardcode absolute URL in WebApplication JSON-LD instead of canonicalUrl — addressed in 7ecbda9, closes #457
- category: engineering

Harmless today since the literal matches `siteConfig.url`, but a future domain change would silently leave stale URLs in the quiz structured data with no test catching it)

- score: 3.6 (impact × ease / 10)
- action: both pages now import `canonicalUrl` from `@thock/seo` and use it in place of the hardcoded literal; output value unchanged (`canonicalUrl` composes `siteConfig.url + path` identically).
- issue: #457

### [x] [bug] [4.5] compare selectors desync from URL on browser back/forward — addressed in 534c826, closes #458
- category: bug
- filed: 2026-07-12 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep)
- impact: 5 (`SwitchCompareSelector.tsx`/`BoardCompareSelector.tsx` seed their `a`/`b` `useState` once from `initialA`/`initialB` props and never resync when those props change on a client-side navigation — the parent server pages render the selector with no `key`, so React reuses the same instance across a `router.push` transition. Reproducible with an ordinary "try pair A, go back, try pair B" exploration on either `/compare/switch` or `/compare/board`: after two Compare clicks and a browser Back, the server-rendered table/H1/JSON-LD correctly revert to the prior pair, but the `<select>` dropdowns still show the second pair — controls actively contradict the rendered content. Touches two full tool page families (phases 44 + 48); self-healing once the user touches a dropdown again, so not a hard block, but genuinely misleading)
- ease: 9 (2-line fix per file — `useEffect(() => setA(initialA), [initialA])` and the `b` equivalent, already-imported `useEffect`; no schema/route change)
- score: 4.5 (impact × ease / 10)
- action: `SwitchCompareSelector.tsx` and `BoardCompareSelector.tsx` gain a `useEffect` resyncing local `a`/`b` state to `initialA`/`initialB` on every prop change. New regression test per component: rerender with new `initialA`/`initialB` props, assert both `<select>` values follow.

### [x] [a11y] [4.5] quiz result rank badge fails WCAG AA color contrast (3.13:1) — addressed in 86e9f7c, closes #459
- category: a11y
- filed: 2026-07-12 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep)
- impact: 5 (`ResultCard.tsx` and `KeycapSetResultCard.tsx` both render the rank number as `text-bg` — dark text — on `bg-accent-mu`, a mid-lightness amber. Verified via OKLCH → linear sRGB → WCAG relative-luminance math on the live tokens: `--thock-bg` (oklch(0.175 0.006 250)) on `--thock-accent-mu` (oklch(0.50 0.07 75)) = 3.13:1, against the WCAG AA 4.5:1 floor for normal text. The badge text is 14px bold, which does not qualify as "large text" (needs ≥18.66px bold), so the stricter threshold applies. Touches both quiz result screens (phases 33 + 47), the terminal step of both quiz flows)
- ease: 9 (one class swap per component — `text-bg` → `text-text`, which computes to 5.48:1 against the same background; no schema/route change)
- score: 4.5 (impact × ease / 10)
- action: swapped `text-bg` → `text-text` in `ResultCard.tsx:34` and `KeycapSetResultCard.tsx:48`. Added a `color-contrast` regression guard in `apps/e2e/tests/a11y.spec.ts` walking `/quiz/switch` to its results screen and scoping axe to `[data-testid="result-card"]`, mirroring the existing per-finding regression-guard convention. Confirmed a candidate fix on the Callout MDX component's `<h2>` title was a false positive — a colocated `spacing.test.tsx` documents that h2 was a deliberate a11y decision (screen-reader heading-jump navigation lands on the callout, chosen to avoid an h1→h3 skip) — reverted before shipping.
- issue: #459
> Picked as the top signal this tick: no unlabeled GitHub issues (triage gate); not Monday (weekly snapshot gate skipped); AUDIT.md and CRITIQUE.md both had zero Pending rows — fully drained; all mechanical surveys clean (content-gap, crosslink, companion, stale-GB, newsletter-gap, OG-coverage, a11y-spec-coverage); no pending phases/data work; march's own expand Step 3c gate not met (5 commits/~3h since pass 181). A fresh general-purpose sweep delegated to a general-purpose agent (5 parallel sub-passes: quiz/compare interactives, packages/*/src test coverage, /data hygiene, SEO+content quality, accessibility) surfaced 5 candidates ≥3.0; the top-scored (Callout `<h2>` title) was verified and found to be an intentional, tested a11y decision rather than a bug, so this — the next-highest genuinely verified finding — shipped instead. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, 608 unit tests, 161 script tests, data:validate (69 records), build, size (108.5 KB/200 KB budget), 996/996 e2e. `pnpm deploy:check` green.

### [x] [bug] [4.2] vendor page group-buy variant misses stale-announced fix — addressed in 99aeaf5, closes #471
- category: engineering
- filed: 2026-07-14 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep)
- impact: 6 (vendor detail pages are a real user-facing surface; a late status flip on any group-buy would render "opens today" instead of a live countdown, contradicting the canonical /group-buys page for the same record)
- ease: 7 (one-line condition change mirroring an existing helper's split, plus a regression test)
- score: 4.2 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: #471
> **Resolved (2026-07-14):** `apps/web/src/components/vendor/VendorGroupBuySection.tsx`'s local `sectionVariant()` helper never got the stale-status fix that `apps/web/src/app/group-buys/helpers.ts`'s `isLive`/`isAnnounced` split already applies (critique #44 precedent). A record left at `status: 'announced'` after its `startDate` has passed rendered unconditionally as `'announced'` — `GroupBuyRow`'s countdown for that variant clamps `dayDelta(today, startDate)` to 0, so the vendor page would show "opens today" for a buy that already opened, while `/group-buys` correctly shows it as live with a countdown to close. Fixed by mirroring the helper's condition: only return `'announced'` while `gb.startDate > today`. Added a regression test (`STALE_ANNOUNCED_GB`) confirming the row now renders `variant="live"` with a correct countdown. No group-buy record in `/data/group-buys` currently triggers this (none are `announced` with a past `startDate` today) — a latent-bug fix, not a live-site correction. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, 609 unit tests, data:validate (70 records), build, 1015/1015 e2e.
> Picked as the top signal this tick: no unlabeled GitHub issues (triage gate); not Monday (weekly snapshot gate skipped); AUDIT.md and CRITIQUE.md both had zero Pending rows; all mechanical surveys clean (content-gap, crosslink, companion, stale-GB, newsletter-gap, OG-coverage, a11y-spec-coverage); no pending phases/data work; march's own expand Step 3c gate not met (5 commits/~3.75h since pass 191, threshold 20 commits/48h). A fresh general-purpose sweep (fact-check pass across ~15 recently-discussed switches/keycap-sets/group-buys against `/data`, plus a spot-check of renderer components handling status/enum fields) found no new content-factual errors but surfaced this renderer bug — a second, independent instance of the exact stale-announced-status class already fixed once on the canonical group-buys page.

### [x] [engineering] [4.8] MDX article internal links render as raw `<a>`, not `next/link` — addressed in 2b6f034, closes #476
- category: engineering
- filed: 2026-07-14 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep) — this exact gap was noted as an unshipped runner-up in the prior tick's audit row (`24401d8`) and re-surfaced here as the top finding
- impact: 6 (every internal cross-link across all 65 articles — 376+ root-relative hrefs — forced a full page reload instead of client-side navigation/prefetching; touches the site's core content-reading surface, though it's a perf/UX regression, not a content-correctness bug)
- ease: 8 (single function in a single file — `AutoLink` in `packages/content/src/mdx/components.tsx` — no data changes, no per-article edits; existing `AutoLink.test.tsx` assertions already exercise the exact `href`/`rel`/`target` contract and needed no rewrite)
- score: 4.8 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: #476
> **Resolved (2026-07-14):** `packages/content/src/mdx/components.tsx`'s `AutoLink` (registered as `mdxComponents.a`, the component every markdown `[text](url)` link in every `.mdx` article compiles through via `next-mdx-remote/rsc`) always rendered a plain `<a href={href}>`, branching only on external-vs-internal for `rel`/`target`, never on which element to render — the one link path on the site that never got the `next/link` treatment already used by Header, Footer, ArticleCard, RelatedArticleCard, etc. Confirmed all internal MDX links are root-relative paths with no hash-fragment or mailto edge cases (`grep`-verified across all article bodies). Added a branch: root-relative internal hrefs now render via `next/link`, external `https?://` hrefs keep the existing anchor with `rel="noopener" target="_blank"`. `next` is already a peer/dev dependency of `@thock/content`. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck (8 workspaces), 613 web + 152 content unit tests, 161 script tests, data:validate (70 records), build, size (108.6 KB/200 KB budget), 1015/1015 e2e. `2b6f034`. Issue #476 was opened after the fix had already pushed (an ordering slip on this tick's Step 2.5 — the finding was picked and shipped in one pass before the mirror step ran); closed manually with a comment pointing at the fix commit rather than via the `Closes #N` trailer.

### [x] [bug] [4.9] /quiz/keycap-set recommends sold-out/discontinued sets over available ones — addressed in 72bf68b, closes #500
- category: bug
- filed: 2026-07-16 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep)
- impact: 7 (first-class, nav-linked, sitemapped interactive tool; 31/108 brute-forced quiz combos surfaced a sold-out/discontinued set in the top-3 even when the user explicitly asked for "in stock now")
- ease: 7 (single-file filter added to recommendKeycapSet.ts, 3 new unit tests)
- score: 4.9 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: #500
> **Resolved (2026-07-16):** Added a pre-filter in `recommendKeycapSet()` — `availabilityPref: 'now'` restricts candidates to `status ∈ {in-stock, group-buy}`; `availabilityPref: 'group-buy'` excludes `status: 'discontinued'` (a discontinued set can never reappear in a group buy). Falls back to the unfiltered catalog only if the filter would empty it, to avoid a dead-end quiz on a thin catalog. 3 new tests reproduce the exact failing combos the audit found. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, 616 web + 121 data + 38 seo + 31 ui + 152 content unit tests, data:validate (71 records), build, size, 1028/1028 e2e.

- issue: #501

Unlike the keycap-set quiz, the switch quiz has no explicit availability question, so the filter applies unconditionally rather than being keyed off an answer. 2 new tests: one constructs a discontinued switch that would otherwise win on every scoring axis and asserts it's excluded; one confirms the all-discontinued fallback returns the unfiltered catalog rather than an empty result.

### [x] [bug] [7.2] Trends Tracker delta sign contradicts the direction arrow on 19 rows — addressed in 02caf48, closes #502
- category: bug
- filed: 2026-07-16 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep, disjoint from passes 195-200 — mobile/responsive, quiz/compare keyboard focus, mentionedParts completeness, compare-tool edge cases, trends-tracker archive integrity, group-buy announced/startDate consistency, newsletter-vs-data staleness, open-issue scan)
- impact: 8 (renders on the homepage Trending strip and every Trends Tracker week page, including the archive — a red down-arrow badge next to a "+28%"-style positive number reads as self-contradictory to any reader, on the site's two highest-traffic surfaces)
- ease: 9 (two near-identical `formatDelta()` functions, one-line sign-source swap in each, plus regression tests)
- score: 7.2 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: #502
> **Resolved (2026-07-16):** Initial hypothesis was a data bug (score's sign should match direction) — disproven by checking `score === spark.at(-1)` across all 197 rows in `data/trends/*.json` (zero exceptions), which shows `score` is a current-level number, not a signed delta, so flipping it would have broken that invariant and misrepresented the real level. The actual bug was in the two `formatDelta()` implementations (`apps/web/src/lib/tracker/index.ts`, `apps/web/src/components/home/TrendingTile.tsx`), which took the +/- prefix from `score`'s own sign instead of the authoritative `direction` field. Fixed both to derive sign from `direction` and magnitude from `Math.abs(score)`; existing tests (which only covered already-consistent score/direction combos) were unaffected, and new regression tests cover the down/positive-score and up/negative-score cases in both files. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, unit tests (94 web test files + others), data:validate, build, size, 1028/1028 e2e.
> Picked as the top signal this tick: no unlabeled GitHub issues (triage gate); not Monday (weekly snapshot gate skipped); AUDIT.md's only other Pending row remains the known-blocked `[6.3]` march.yml permission item; CRITIQUE.md's only Pending row remains the non-actionable `[needs-user-call]` GA-beacon item; all 7 mechanical surveys clean (content-gap, crosslink, group-buy-companion, group-buy-status, newsletter-gap, OG-coverage, a11y-spec-coverage); no pending phases/data work; march's own expand Step 3c gate not met (5 commits/~10h since pass 200, threshold 20 commits/48h). A fresh general-purpose sweep across 8 areas disjoint from passes 195-200 plus this tick's own two earlier quiz fixes surfaced this as the one clear, verified, highest-scoring finding — also confirmed GH issue #395 (march.yml crash-gate) is still legitimately open/blocked and a minor newsletter factual-staleness item too low-stakes (archived issue) to action this tick; other candidate areas (mobile/responsive, compare-tool edge cases, group-buy announced/startDate) came back clean.

### [x] [seo] [3.6] search index missing newsletters, vendors, and group buys — addressed in this commit, closes #503
- category: seo
- filed: 2026-07-16 by cloud /iterate audit (resurfaced from a 2026-07-15 pass's footnote — noted then as a "real functional gap, worth a follow-up tick" but never formalized as its own row or shipped)
- impact: 6 (`/search` is a first-class, header-linked discovery surface; `apps/web/scripts/generate-search-index.mts` only ever indexed articles + switches/keycap-sets/boards, so a reader typing a vendor name like "CannonKeys" or a newsletter reference like "issue 03" got zero results despite both having dedicated detail pages — `/vendor/[slug]` and `/newsletter/[slug]` respectively)
- ease: 6 (mechanical extension of the existing `parts` catalog pattern in the generator + runtime type + `PartResult` component, but group-buys have no per-slug detail route, so routing them correctly required mirroring `getAllClosedGroupBuys()`'s status/endDate logic and adding an anchor `id` to `GroupBuyRow` for `/group-buys#slug` / `/group-buys/past#slug` deep links)
- score: 3.6 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: #503
> **Resolved (2026-07-16):** Extended `generate-search-index.mts`'s `parts` catalog with vendors (`getAllVendors`), newsletters (`getAllNewsletters`), and group buys (`getAllGroupBuys`), each carrying an explicit `href` computed at index-build time rather than reconstructed client-side. Vendors → `/vendor/[slug]`; newsletters → `/newsletter/[slug]`; group buys → `/group-buys#slug` (live/announced) or `/group-buys/past#slug` (closed/shipped, or live/announced past `endDate` — same test `isGroupBuyPast()` mirrors `getAllClosedGroupBuys()`). Added `id={groupBuy.slug}` to `<GroupBuyRow>`'s outer `<article>` so both group-buy surfaces are deep-linkable. `PartResult.tsx` now renders via `hit.href` instead of reconstructing `/part/[kind]/[slug]`, with new "Vendor" / "Newsletter" / "Group Buy" kind labels. Renamed the `/search` results section label from "parts" to "catalog" since it's no longer parts-only. New/updated tests: `PartResult.test.tsx` (3 new kind cases), `runtime.test.ts` (2 new cases covering all three new kinds + past-archive routing), `GroupBuyRow.test.tsx` (anchor id). `pnpm verify` full gate green.

### [x] [data] [6.4] durock-t1 spring spec wrong: 67g/70g should be 55g/67g — addressed in 5cd11fd, closes #508
- category: data
- filed: 2026-07-17 by cloud /iterate audit (general-purpose sub-agent sweep disjoint from passes 196-200: re-verified 5 recent bug-fix commits, the new wooting-60he board record, the new search-index extension, and a fresh numeric-spec spot-check)
- impact: 8 (`data/switches/durock-t1.json` specs the T1 at 67g actuation / 70g bottom-out; independently verified against 5 vendor sources — ThockKing, Divinikey, MechanicalKeyboards.com, Upgrade Keyboards, Mechboards UK — the real spec is 55g actuation / 67g bottom-out, with "67g" in the marketing name referring to bottom-out per Durock/JWK convention, not actuation. `durock-t1-deep-dive.mdx`'s central comparative thesis — "the T1 is the heaviest spring in the set" — was built on the wrong numbers across 6 passages, and two hand-authored SVGs baked the wrong figures into rendered `<text>` labels)
- ease: 8 (one JSON field correction + article rewrite of the affected comparative passages + SVG text-label edits; no schema change, no new records)
- score: 6.4 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: #508
> **Resolved (2026-07-17):** Corrected `data/switches/durock-t1.json` springGrams to `{actuation: 55, bottomOut: 67}`. Rewrote `durock-t1-deep-dive.mdx`'s comparative framing — with the correct spec the T1 is no longer the heaviest raw spring in the set (the U4T's 62g actuation is heavier); its actual distinguishing trait is the widest actuation-to-bottom-out spread (12g vs. 6-7g for U4T/Lanes/HPX). Corrected 6 prose passages plus the InlineViz alt text and caption. Edited baked `<text>` labels in `spring-and-bump-ladder.svg` and `mechanism-comparison.svg` (and their provenance `.json` sidecars) to match. `manifest.generated.json` / `og-manifest.generated.json` / `search/index.generated.json` regenerated via standard prebuild. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, unit tests, data:validate, build, 1031/1031 e2e.

### [x] [seo] [4.8] part pages emit invalid Product JSON-LD with no offers/review/rating — addressed in this commit, closes #519
- category: seo
- filed: 2026-07-17 by cloud /iterate audit
- impact: 6 (`apps/web/src/app/part/[kind]/[slug]/page.tsx:135` declared `@type: Product` for all 18 switch + 10 keycap-set part pages; Google's structured-data guidelines require a `Product` type to carry at least one of `offers`/`review`/`aggregateRating`, and neither the switch nor keycap-set schema carries any price/rating field to build them from — 28 URLs surface as invalid Product markup in Search Console. Board pages already sidestepped this correctly by using `Thing`)
- ease: 8 (one-line `@type` ternary change to make switch/keycap-set consistent with board's existing `Thing`; two e2e assertions updated to match — `meta.spec.ts`'s `expectedTypesFor` and `parts.spec.ts`'s type-shape test/title, no data migration)
- score: 4.8 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: #519
> **Resolved (2026-07-17):** Changed `page.tsx:135`'s `'@type': part.kind === 'board' ? 'Thing' : 'Product'` to a flat `'@type': 'Thing'` — all three part kinds now emit the same schema.org type, since none carry commerce data. Kept the `brand` field (harmless descriptive metadata under `Thing`, not schema-invalid — only `Product`'s specific required-property check was the actual violation). Updated `meta.spec.ts`'s `expectedTypesFor()` for `/part/(switch|keycap-set)/*` from `['Product', 'BreadcrumbList']` to `['Thing', 'BreadcrumbList']`; updated `parts.spec.ts`'s JSON-LD shape test and brand-name test titles/assertions to match the new always-`Thing` reality (no more `Product|Thing` either/or). Found via a fresh general-purpose audit sweep across RSS freshness, sitemap lastmod, quiz/compare edge cases, structured-data validation, PWA/favicon coverage, dark mode, and footer/nav integrity — all other areas came back clean. `pnpm verify` full gate green.

### [x] [a11y] [4.2] quiz question transitions drop keyboard focus with no announcement — addressed in 7421b6b, closes #521
- category: a11y
- filed: 2026-07-17 by cloud /iterate audit (general-purpose fresh sweep)
- impact: 6 (`/quiz/switch` and `/quiz/keycap-set` — both flagship interactive tools, nav-linked, sitemap priority 0.7 — advance to the next question by unmounting the option `<button>`s, each keyed by `opt.value` which differs per question, and mounting a fresh set 150ms later. The previously-focused button unmounts, so browser focus silently reverts to `<body>`; no `aria-live` region exists anywhere in `QuizStep.tsx`/`QuizProgress.tsx`. A keyboard or screen-reader user gets zero signal that a new question loaded and must re-tab from the top of the page after every single answer)
- ease: 7 (self-contained fix in the shared `QuizStep.tsx` component — one ref, one effect, one `tabIndex={-1}` — covers both `SwitchQuiz.tsx` and `KeycapSetQuiz.tsx` since they both render through it; test scaffolding already existed for all four affected files)
- score: 4.2 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: #521
> **Resolved (2026-07-17):** Added a ref + `tabIndex={-1}` to the question `<h2>` in `QuizStep.tsx`, and an effect that moves focus to it whenever the `question` prop changes — skipping the initial mount (via an `isFirstRender` ref) so page load doesn't get an unexpected focus jump. Moving focus to the new heading both restores a sane keyboard-focus position and gets the new question announced by assistive tech via the standard focus-change event; no separate `aria-live` region needed. 3 new tests in `QuizStep.test.tsx`: no focus steal on initial mount, focus moves to the new heading on question change, heading carries `tabIndex={-1}`. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, 632 web unit tests + 161 script tests, data:validate (72 records), build, bundle size OK, 1033/1033 e2e.
> Picked as the top signal this tick: no unlabeled GitHub issues (triage gate); not Monday (weekly snapshot gate skipped); AUDIT.md's only other Pending row remains the known-blocked `[6.3]` march.yml crash-issue-gate item; CRITIQUE.md's only Pending row remains the non-actionable `[needs-user-call]` GA-beacon item; all 7 mechanical surveys clean (content-gap, crosslink, group-buy-companion, group-buy-status, newsletter-gap, OG-coverage, a11y-spec-coverage); no pending phases/data/content-gap work; march's own expand Step 3c gate not met (7 commits/~4.6h since pass 203, threshold 20 commits/48h). A fresh general-purpose sweep (link integrity, a11y, tests, SEO/JSON-LD elsewhere, data cross-refs, dead code/regression scan across the last 8 commits) found this quiz focus-management bug as the one finding above the 3.0 threshold; a runner-up (compare-table `<dt>`/`<dd>` wiring, score 2.0) and a handful of dead-code/unused-export items (all sub-3.0, no user-facing impact) were sub-threshold and left unaddressed.

New regression test in `MobileNav.test.tsx` opens the drawer, focuses the first link, fires an Escape keydown, and asserts `document.activeElement` returns to the toggle. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, unit tests, data:validate, build, 1033/1033 e2e.

### [x] [bug] [5.6] 4 of 6 dynamic entity routes soft-404 (HTTP 200) on unknown slugs — 4/6 addressed in this commit, closes #524
- category: bug
- filed: 2026-07-18 by cloud /iterate audit (general-purpose fresh sweep)
- impact: 8 (`/vendor/[slug]`, `/article/[slug]`, `/tag/[slug]`, `/newsletter/[slug]`, `/part/[kind]`, `/part/[kind]/[slug]` all call `notFound()` in-page for unknown slugs, but the HTTP response status was 200, not 404 — confirmed live on thock.xyz via curl for all six. `/trends/tracker/[week]` was the sole exception, already correctly 404ing since phase 27/31 shipped it with `dynamicParams = false` + `generateStaticParams`. Soft-404s risk Search Console flags and wasted crawl budget across every mistyped/removed catalog, article, tag, or newsletter URL sitewide)
- ease: 7 (the working `trends/tracker/[week]` pattern already exists in-repo; mechanical to replicate on 4 of the 6 routes — the remaining 2, `/article/[slug]` and `/tag/[slug]`, have a `not-found.tsx` that reads `headers()` for "did you mean" slug suggestions, which forces dynamic rendering and is incompatible with `dynamicParams = false`'s build-time rejection, so those two need a separate fix)
- score: 5.6 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: #524
> **Resolved (2026-07-18), partial:** Added `export const dynamicParams = false` + `generateStaticParams` to `vendor/[slug]/page.tsx`, `newsletter/[slug]/page.tsx`, `part/[kind]/page.tsx`, and `part/[kind]/[slug]/page.tsx` — all four now return a real 404 for unknown slugs (verified via local `next build && next start` curl: all four flip from 200 to 404, matching the `trends/tracker/[week]` control case). Note this makes those routes' own nested `not-found.tsx` files unreachable (the routing layer rejects the request before the segment renders, falling back to the root `not-found.tsx`) — this is the same pre-existing trade-off `trends/tracker/[week]/not-found.tsx` already accepted; left the now-dead files in place rather than expanding this tick's scope to delete them. Added `generateStaticParams` to `article/[slug]/page.tsx` and `tag/[slug]/page.tsx` too (a free SSG win — valid slugs now prerender), but deliberately did **not** add `dynamicParams = false` to either: both routes' `not-found.tsx` call `headers()` to power "did you mean" suggestions (`SuggestedArticles`), which forces dynamic rendering of the 404 boundary and, tested directly, produces a broken half-streamed 200 response when combined with `dynamicParams = false` — worse than the pre-existing soft-404. Filed as the standing gap on this same issue for a future tick: making `SuggestedArticles`' pathname detection client-side (`usePathname()`) instead of the middleware-injected `x-pathname` header would remove the `headers()` dependency and unblock applying the same fix there. Updated `apps/e2e/tests/newsletter.spec.ts`'s stale not-found test (previously asserted the old 200 + custom-copy behavior, now asserts a real 404 status). `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, 633 unit tests, 161 script tests, data:validate (72 records), build, size, 1033/1033 e2e.
> Picked as the top signal this tick: no unlabeled GitHub issues (triage gate); not Monday (weekly snapshot gate skipped); AUDIT.md's only other Pending row remains the known-blocked `[6.3]` march.yml crash-issue-gate item; CRITIQUE.md's only Pending row remains the non-actionable `[needs-user-call]` GA-beacon item; all 7 mechanical surveys clean; no pending phases/data/content-gap work; march's own expand Step 3c gate not met (13 commits/~8.5h since pass 203, threshold 20 commits/48h). A fresh general-purpose sweep (compare tool, search, meta description lengths, Twitter/OG completeness, robots.txt, PWA manifest, 404 pages per entity family, loading states, redirects, ItemList `numberOfItems` truthfulness, recently-shipped-component leftover checks, data cross-ref integrity) found this soft-404 pattern as the one finding above the 3.0 threshold, with a live production reproduction on all 6 routes.

- impact: 7 (flagship, nav-linked, sitemapped quiz tool; brute-forcing all 36 profile × material × legend combos with `availabilityPref: 'no-pref'` against the real 10-record catalog surfaced a sold-out/discontinued set in the top 3 for 35/36 combos (97%), and as the #1 recommendation for 18/36 (50%); `KeycapSetResultCard` carries no status badge, so the reader has no signal until clicking through to `/part/keycap-set/[slug]`)

- issue: #525
> **Resolved (2026-07-18):** `filterByAvailability()`'s `no-pref` branch in `recommendKeycapSet.ts` returned the full unfiltered catalog — the only one of the three branches with no status filter. Filtered it the same way as `now`/`group-buy`: excludes `discontinued` and `sold-out`, falling back to the unfiltered catalog only if that would empty the eligible set. Added regression test K reproducing the failing combo (mirrors tests H/I). Test B's premise turned out to rely on the catalog's only `sa`-profile fixture happening to be discontinued — previously masked because the no-pref branch didn't filter; added an in-stock `sa` fixture (`SA_AVAILABLE`) so profile-matching and availability-filtering are tested independently going forward. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, 634 web unit tests, data:validate (72 records), build, 1033/1033 e2e.

- impact: 7 (`PartSpec.tsx` — the canonical `/part/[kind]/[slug]` detail page, 38 sitemapped catalog URLs — and both `SwitchCompareTable.tsx`/`BoardCompareTable.tsx` (`/compare/switch`, `/compare/board`, flagship nav-linked tools) rendered several spec fields as raw schema enum values: switch `housingTop: 'pc'` printed "pc" not "PC"; board `layout: '60'` printed "60" not "60%"; `caseMaterial: 'fr4'` printed "fr4" not "FR4"; `mountStyle: 'top-mount'` printed "top-mount" not "Top mount"; keycap-set `profile: 'mt3'` printed "mt3" not "MT3". The codebase already solves this exact pattern elsewhere — `VendorBoardSection.tsx`'s `LAYOUT_LABEL`, `KeycapSetResultCard.tsx`'s `PROFILE_LABEL`/`MATERIAL_LABEL`, and both compare tables' own `STATUS_LABEL` (with an explicit "human label, not raw enum" regression test) — but left these fields unmapped)
- ease: 8 (mechanical — one shared label-mapping module plus three call-site edits, no schema/data change; existing per-component precedent made the map values easy to source correctly)
- score: 5.6 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: #528

Added regression tests in all three components' test files plus a new `spec-labels.test.ts`, mirroring the existing "renders the status as a human label, not the raw enum value" precedent test. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, 806 unit tests, 161 script tests, data:validate (72 records), build, size (108.6 KB/200 KB budget), 1033/1033 e2e. `pnpm deploy:check` green (dpl_zst24D9n, 94s to ready).

### [ ] [engineering] [4.0] Lighthouse CI silently disabled + failing on every recorded run since ~2026-06-12 — the site's only automated a11y/perf/SEO regression gate has been dark for 5+ weeks with no trail explaining why
- category: engineering
- filed: 2026-07-18 by /digest nightly breadth pass
- impact: 8 (`.github/workflows/lighthouse.yml` is the only automated check on production a11y/performance/SEO scores — every other gate in this repo checks internal correctness, not real Lighthouse audits. Step 2's `gh run list --workflow lighthouse -L 2 --json conclusion` pulse command has been erroring "could not find any workflows named lighthouse" on every digest run since at least 2026-07-15, and each prior digest logged that error as "nothing to report" and moved on. It does not mean the workflow doesn't exist: `gh api repos/daretodave/thock/actions/workflows` shows `{"name":"lighthouse","path":".github/workflows/lighthouse.yml","state":"disabled_manually"}` — `gh run list` silently can't resolve a disabled workflow by display name (`--workflow lighthouse.yml`, by filename, works fine and returns real history). Of the last 100 recorded runs, 98 are `failure` and 2 are `skipped` — zero recorded successes in that window. The most recent run before it went dark (`27515188524`, 2026-06-14T23:15:49Z) failed on a real regression (`categories.seo` 0.92, required ≥0.95) and a real a11y hit (`label-content-name-mismatch` — visible text labels without matching accessible names, scored 0), plus a batch of `minScore` failures against newer Lighthouse "Insight" audits (`lcp-discovery-insight`, `network-dependency-tree-insight`, `legacy-javascript-insight`) that don't appear to emit conventional 0–1 category scores under the pinned `treosh/lighthouse-ci-action` version — so at least part of the failure signal is `.lighthouserc.json` assertion drift against a newer Lighthouse audit catalog, not all real regressions. `disabled_manually` (as opposed to GitHub's `disabled_inactivity` auto-disable state) means a human or an out-of-band API call explicitly turned this off — there is no commit, AUDIT.md row, or PHASE_CANDIDATES.md note in this repo's history documenting that action or its reasoning.)
- ease: 5 (two independent, moderate-size fixes: (1) `.lighthouserc.json`'s assertion set needs auditing against the current Lighthouse/treosh action version to drop or reclassify the "Insight"-audit assertions that no longer emit a comparable `minScore`; this file is not under `.github/workflows/`, so a cloud tick can push it. (2) the workflow itself needs re-enabling via `gh workflow enable lighthouse.yml` or the Actions UI — a reversible, low-risk action, but one a human deliberately took once already with no recorded reason, which is why this is filed rather than just re-enabled inline by this digest tick.)
- score: 4.0 (impact × ease / 10)
- next: `/oversight` call on (1) whether to re-enable now or investigate the original disable reason first; (2) once live, fix `.lighthouserc.json`'s assertion set against the current Lighthouse audit catalog; (3) address the two real hits from the last completed run (`categories.seo` 0.92, `label-content-name-mismatch`).
- issue: (none filed by this digest — filing GitHub issues is a shipping-skill action, not digest's; the next `/iterate` or `/oversight` pass that picks this row up opens one)
> Discovered while gathering the nightly pulse (skills/digest.md Step 2's `gh run list --workflow lighthouse` command) — the error message every prior digest logged turned out to mask a real, silently-disabled CI gate rather than a nonexistent workflow. Flagged loudly in this tick's `plan/DIGEST.md` "Needs you" section; not shipped by this digest (breadth-check findings become AUDIT rows, not fixes, per `skills/digest.md` §4.2).

### [x] [seo] [4.5] vendor detail Organization JSON-LD swaps url and sameAs — addressed in 7539cd3, closes #531
- category: seo
- filed: 2026-07-18 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep)
- impact: 5 (`apps/web/src/app/vendor/[slug]/page.tsx`'s `Organization` JSON-LD assigned `url: canonicalUrl(path)` — thock's own page about the vendor — and `sameAs: vendor.url` — the vendor's actual homepage. Backwards per schema.org/Google Organization convention (`url` = entity's own site, `sameAs` = alternate identity profiles), and inconsistent with the page's own rendered outbound link a few lines below, which already treats `vendor.url` as the vendor's real external site. Wrong on all 10 `/vendor/<slug>` pages — a small structured-data correctness bug, not user-visible)
- ease: 9 (two-line swap plus an unused-import removal; no schema/component dependency; no existing test covered the JSON-LD block's field values)
- score: 4.5 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: #531
> **Resolved (2026-07-18):** swapped `url: vendor.url` in for the incorrect `canonicalUrl(path)` value and dropped the backwards `sameAs`; removed the now-unused `canonicalUrl` import. New e2e assertion in `vendors.spec.ts` pins the Organization node's `url` to the vendor's real homepage (`https://cannonkeys.com`) and asserts no `sameAs`. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, 648 web unit tests, 161 script tests, data:validate (72 records), build, size (108.6 KB/200 KB budget), 1033/1033 e2e.
> Picked as the top signal this tick: no unlabeled GitHub issues (triage gate); not Monday (weekly snapshot gate skipped); AUDIT.md's only other Pending row is the `[4.0]` Lighthouse-CI-disabled `/oversight` item (filed by /digest, needs-user-call — re-enabling a workflow a human deliberately turned off isn't an autonomous call) and the known-blocked `[6.3]` march.yml crash-issue-gate permission item; CRITIQUE.md's only Pending row remains the non-actionable `[needs-user-call]` GA-beacon item; all 7 mechanical surveys clean (content-gap, crosslink, group-buy-companion, group-buy-status, newsletter-gap, OG-coverage, a11y-spec-coverage); no pending phases/data/content-gap work; march's own expand Step 3c gate not met (6 commits/~5h54m since pass 204, threshold 20 commits/48h). A fresh general-purpose sweep (external link rot, content-fact-vs-catalog numeric spec checks across 9 switch deep-dives, stale group-buy prose across all 13 closed group-buy articles, JSON-LD across compare/vendors/archive/tools/quiz, focus-visible on newer interactive surfaces) found link rot, numeric facts, and stale prose all clean; this JSON-LD field-swap was the one verified, scoreable defect.

### [needs-user-call] [bug] [4.2] `/article/[slug]` and `/tag/[slug]` soft-404 fix is structurally blocked — real 404 status and the "did you mean" not-found UX are mutually exclusive under current Next.js routing
- category: bug
- filed: 2026-07-18 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep, then investigated + reverted same tick)
- impact: 7 (same impact reasoning as the closed-out #524 partial fix: these are the two highest-traffic dynamic route families, and soft-404s risk Search Console flags/wasted crawl budget)
- ease: 6 (originally scored — the fix looked mechanical: convert `article/[slug]/not-found.tsx` and `tag/[slug]/not-found.tsx` from reading `headers()` server-side to `usePathname()` client-side, then add `dynamicParams = false`. Implemented and verified directly against a local `next build && next start`: confirmed this does NOT work. With `dynamicParams` at the default (`true`), unknown slugs render the segment's own `not-found.tsx` — "did you mean" suggestions intact — but HTTP status is 200 (the pre-existing soft-404). With `dynamicParams = false`, unknown slugs correctly 404 — confirmed via curl — but Next.js bypasses the segment's own `not-found.tsx` boundary entirely and falls back to the root `not-found.tsx` ("Lost in the layout."), losing the suggestions; this happens regardless of whether the not-found page reads the pathname server-side or client-side, because the routing-layer rejection happens before the segment boundary is considered at all. `apps/e2e/tests/polish.spec.ts`'s two "did you mean" tests caught this immediately when the change was verify-gated. The two properties (accurate 404 status, segment-level "did you mean" UX) are confirmed mutually exclusive for these two routes under the current Next.js version — same trade-off already accepted for `/vendor/[slug]`/`/newsletter/[slug]`/`/part/[kind]` in #524, but those routes had no "did you mean" feature to lose, so it was free there. Here it isn't — an autonomous ease score can't be assigned to "which regression do we prefer")
- score: 4.2 (impact × ease / 10, as originally computed before the ease reassessment above — carried forward for continuity with the closed issue, not a live autonomous priority)
- next: `/oversight` call on which is preferred: (a) keep the current soft-404 (200 status, working "did you mean" suggestions) — the status quo since #524; or (b) accept `dynamicParams = false` (real 404, but the not-found page falls back to generic root copy with no suggestions) — same trade-off already made for the other 4 entity routes. A third option — building the "did you mean" suggestions into the *root* `not-found.tsx` with route-aware behavior — wasn't scoped in this investigation and could be a future `/plan-a-phase` candidate if (b) is preferred but the UX loss is unacceptable.
- issue: #533 (opened, investigated same tick, closed not-planned — see issue comments for the full local-repro evidence trail)
> Filed same-tick as investigated: the fresh sweep's top finding (score 4.2) matched a standing follow-up gap from #524's partial resolution, was implemented, and failed its own e2e coverage (`polish.spec.ts`'s two "did you mean" assertions) on the first `pnpm verify` pass. Rather than iterate blindly against a gate that was correctly catching a real, structural regression, did a direct local repro (`next build && next start` + curl, both `dynamicParams` states) to confirm the constraint before reverting — this is a routing-layer limitation, not an implementation bug, so further iterate-tick attempts at "just read the pathname differently" would repeat this same dead end. All file changes reverted to a clean working tree; no commit shipped this line of investigation. Falls through to the next candidate finding for this tick.

### [x] [bug] [4.5] root error.tsx claims every route is the home page — addressed in ca326b5
- category: bug
- filed: 2026-07-18 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep)
- impact: 5 (`apps/web/src/app/error.tsx` is Next.js's fallback error boundary for every route segment without its own `error.tsx` — verified ~19 route families rely on it: `/`, `/about`, `/search`, `/sources`, `/compare`, `/compare/switch`, `/compare/board`, `/quiz`, `/quiz/switch`, `/quiz/keycap-set`, `/tools`, `/newsletter`, `/vendor/[slug]`, `/vendors`, `/part/[kind]`, `/part/[kind]/[slug]`, `/parts`, `/archive`, `/feed/[pillar]`. It was hardcoded `HomeError` with copy "Something went wrong loading the home page." and a "← about thock →" link — misleading on every route other than "/", including the worst case of firing on `/about` itself and linking back to the page that just errored. Every other route family with a local `error.tsx` (news, trends, guides, ideas, deep-dives, group-buys, tag, article) already uses scoped, route-agnostic-safe copy)
- ease: 9 (single-file text/copy change — genericize heading and kicker, swap the `/about` link for the same "← back to home" pattern every other local `error.tsx` already uses; no logic change)
- score: 4.5 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: (not mirrored — iterate Step 2.5 mirror was skipped this tick; fix shipped directly)
> **Resolved (2026-07-18):** renamed `HomeError` → `RootError`, changed the kicker from "error · home" to "error", the heading to "Something went wrong loading this page.", and the escape link from `/about` ("about thock →") to `/` ("← back to home") — matching the exact pattern root `not-found.tsx` already documents and uses for the same shared-fallback role. Added a doc comment explaining which routes rely on this boundary. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, 664 web unit tests, data:validate (72 records), build, 1037/1037 e2e.
> Picked as the top signal this tick: no unlabeled GitHub issues (triage gate); not Monday (weekly snapshot gate skipped); AUDIT.md's only other Pending rows are the `[6.3]` blocked-cloud-permission march.yml item and the `[4.0]` Lighthouse-CI `/oversight` item, both explicitly non-autonomous; CRITIQUE.md's only Pending row remains the non-actionable `[needs-user-call]` GA-beacon item; all 7 mechanical surveys clean (content-gap, crosslink, group-buy-companion, group-buy-status, newsletter-gap, OG-coverage, a11y-spec-coverage); no pending phases/data/content-gap work; march's own expand Step 3c gate not met (14 commits/~16h since pass 204, threshold 20 commits/48h). ### [x] [external-issue] [4.2] deploy:check never ingests a follow-up commit pushed seconds after a prior push (Vercel webhook drop on rapid double-push) — addressed in 350f623, closes #540
- category: external-issue
- filed: 2026-07-19 via /triage from user-issue #540 (opened by cloud /march tick)
- impact: 6 (a commit pushed to main can silently have no Vercel deployment record at all — not queued, not building, not errored, just absent — which would let a genuine code regression ship with deploy:check falsely timing out and masking the real state; confirmed reproducing: HEAD 09ab17a still has zero deployment 40+ min after push, past the 10-min deploy:check timeout)
- ease: 7 (scoped fix: merge the fix-commit and the audit-follow-up commit into a single push in the shipping skills' Step 6/7 — e.g. skills/iterate.md — so two rapid-fire pushes ~20s apart don't double-trigger the git-integration webhook; no Vercel-side access needed)
- score: 4.2 (impact × ease / 10)
- next: /iterate to merge the trailing plan/AUDIT.md-only follow-up commit into the same push as the fix commit in skills/iterate.md (and any other shipping skill with a similar two-commit Step 6/7 shape); reference #540 in the commit body.
- issue: #540
> **Resolved (2026-07-19):** `skills/iterate.md` (Step 5/6), `skills/ship-a-phase.md` (Step 10/11), `skills/ship-content.md` (Step 7d/8), and `skills/march.md` (Step 0.5's trend-snapshot flow) all had the same two-commit shape where the first commit pushed immediately and a trailing audit/DoD-tick commit pushed again seconds later. Each now stages its trailing commit locally and pushes once, after both commits exist — new Hard rules document the pattern in each skill (iterate.md §7.8, ship-a-phase.md §7.12, ship-content.md §5.8). `skills/ship-content.md`'s Step 3a early issue-number checkpoint was deliberately left alone — it's a resumability checkpoint spanning minutes of agent work (content-curator/brander), not a rapid-fire pair, so collapsing it into a later push would reintroduce the crash-recovery gap phase 24 built it to close. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, unit, data:validate (72 records), build, 1055/1055 e2e. This tick's own two commits (`350f623` fix + this audit-tick commit) are pushed together as one push, dogfooding the fix immediately.
> Filed 2026-07-19 by /triage. User-issue #540 self-reported by the prior cloud /march tick: `687c11c` (content fix, pushed 18:30:31Z) got a Vercel deployment and went READY in ~4s; `09ab17a` (audit-only follow-up, pushed 18:30:53Z, 22s later) never got one. Site is unaffected (687c11c is live) but main's HEAD has no deploy record tracking it — a risk if a future rapid-fire second push carries an actual code change.

- ease: 9 (single JSON `note` field rewrite in one file; no schema/component/article change needed since the article's own count was already correct)
- score: 4.5 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: #544

A fresh general-purpose sweep (newsletter numeric cross-check, external citation sample, RSS/archive/sources correctness, last-10-commit diff review, and full data-provenance trace of the new prototypist-vendor-spotlight article) initially proposed rewriting the article's own "three" to "two" to match the tracker row — direct verification of the underlying per-project rows for W25 (Selene closing June 19, Pandemonium through July 1, Masterpiece R2 active June 5–26, all naming Prototypist) showed the *tracker row* undercounted, not the article; corrected the data instead of introducing a factual error into the article.

### [x] [a11y] [4.8] Quiz results/reset transitions drop focus to `<body>` — third instance of a known focus-loss pattern — addressed in this commit, closes #546
- category: a11y
- filed: 2026-07-20 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep, pass 209)
- impact: 6 (`apps/web/src/components/quiz/SwitchQuiz.tsx` and `KeycapSetQuiz.tsx` both unmount the active `<QuizStep>` heading when the quiz completes, and again unmount the results view when "Start over" is clicked, with nothing receiving focus either time — keyboard/screen-reader users are silently dropped to `<body>` at both transitions. Two prior fixes already established this as a recurring class: MobileNav Escape-close and `QuizStep`'s own question-to-question transition (`headingRef` + `tabIndex={-1}` + focus-on-question-change). This is a third, previously-uncaught pair of instances in the same quiz surfaces.)
- ease: 8 (mirror the existing `QuizStep` heading-focus pattern: focus the results `<h2>` on completion; add an `autoFocus` prop to `QuizStep` so the post-reset first-question heading also receives focus, since a freshly-mounted `QuizStep` instance otherwise treats itself as a first render and skips the auto-focus)
- score: 4.8 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: #546
> **Resolved (2026-07-20):** `QuizStep.tsx` gained an optional `autoFocus` prop that, when set, overrides the existing first-render skip so a freshly-mounted instance still focuses its heading. `SwitchQuiz.tsx` and `KeycapSetQuiz.tsx` each gained a `resultsHeadingRef` (focused via `useEffect` on `isDone` becoming true) and a `hasCompletedRef` (set once the quiz first completes, passed as `QuizStep`'s `autoFocus` prop) so the post-reset first question heading focuses too. 4 new unit tests (2 per file) cover both transitions. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, 671 web unit tests (+2 in each of `SwitchQuiz.test.tsx`/`KeycapSetQuiz.test.tsx`, +1 in `QuizStep.test.tsx`), 161 script tests, data:validate (74 records), build, size (108.6 KB/200 KB budget), 1064/1064 e2e.
> Picked as the top signal this tick: no unlabeled GitHub issues; Monday snapshot gate already satisfied; no pending phases/data/content-gap work (content-gap-survey: all pillars comfortable; all 7 companion surveys clean too); march's expand Step 3c gate not due (5 commits since pass 206, ~4h elapsed, well under the 20-commit/48h threshold). AUDIT.md's only other Pending rows remain the `[6.3]` blocked-cloud-permission and `[4.0]` Lighthouse-CI items, both non-autonomous; CRITIQUE.md's only Pending row remains the non-actionable GA-beacon item. A fresh general-purpose sweep (focus management across modals/quiz/compare/tracker, TS escape-hatch grep beyond `as any`, orphaned-export scan, numeric-fact spot-check against catalog records, fresh external citation sample, `/compare`+`/search` malformed-query-param edge cases, TODO/FIXME grep, frontmatter cruft census) surfaced this as the top finding — a genuine third instance completing the focus-loss pattern the prior two fixes only partially closed — plus two sub-3.0 findings held for a future bundle: dead `readTimeMinutes`/`wordCount`/`readingTime` frontmatter on 26 articles (3.2, same class as the already-Pending dead-prop/schema-cleanup candidate) and `PillarOG.tsx`'s `Record<string, string>` tagline map forcing unchecked `!` at 5 call sites (3.2, first instance — not yet a pattern, noted for a future candidate if it recurs).

- ease: 8 (all patched versions satisfy existing semver ranges for most packages; js-yaml and @babel/core needed exact-version pins after an open-range override jumped them to breaking majors in local testing)

### [x] [bug] [4.5] Trends Tracker header hardcodes "of 52" weeks, breaks in 2026's 53-week year — addressed in 23946fc, closes #552
- category: bug
- filed: 2026-07-20 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep, pass 214)
- impact: 5 (`TrackerHeader.tsx` renders a fixed "of 52" label next to the big week number on both `/trends/tracker` and every `/trends/tracker/[week]` archive page (12+ pages today, one more every Monday). 2026 is an ISO 53-week year (confirmed via the standard ISO week-count formula and cross-checked against Python's `datetime.isocalendar()`); the site autonomously publishes a snapshot every Monday, so the December 2026 W53 snapshot would render the self-contradictory "53 · of 52" on the tracker pillar's flagship surface and archive it permanently at `/trends/tracker/2026-W53`)
- ease: 9 (one hardcoded string in one component; new pure `isoWeeksInYear(year)` helper alongside the existing `weekKicker` in the same lib file; no schema/route/content change)
- score: 4.5 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: #552
> **Resolved (2026-07-20):** added `isoWeeksInYear(year: number): number` to `apps/web/src/lib/tracker/index.ts` using the standard ISO long-year formula; `TrackerHeader.tsx` now renders `of {isoWeeksInYear(week.year)}` instead of the literal `of 52`. Unit tests pin 2026/2020 → 53 and 2025/2027 → 52; a `TrackerHeader` test asserts "of 53" renders for a `2026-W53` snapshot. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, unit, data:validate, build, 1064/1064 e2e.

A fresh general-purpose sweep (recent-commit regression check, tracker archive sparkline math, quiz recommender edge cases, search index coverage, newsletter archive continuity, data schema soft-invariants, `/parts` and `/tools` blurb accuracy, dependency freshness) found this ISO-53-week-year defect as the one genuinely new, verified, currently-latent-but-certain-to-manifest correctness bug.

### [x] [content] [5.4] group-buy articles' vendor-link disclosure omits named-part links, self-contradicted by their own PartReference markup — addressed in d471210, closes #555
- category: content
- filed: 2026-07-21 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep, pass 208)
- impact: 6 (`mode-sonnet-r2-group-buy-coverage.mdx`, `divinikey-dcs-dolch-group-buy.mdx`, and `gmk-cyl-masterpiece-r2-group-buy.mdx` each carry a "note on links" disclosure sentence claiming only the "view at vendor" button on `/group-buys` gets auto-flagged `rel="sponsored noopener"` — "not citation links inside article bodies." That's a false dichotomy: `packages/content/src/mdx/PartReference.tsx:39-47` unconditionally applies `rel="sponsored noopener"` to any resolved `<PartReference>` (a named-part mention that resolves to a vendor URL), and each of these three articles uses `<PartReference>` itself, resolving to a real vendor link (`mode-sonnet-r2-group-buy-coverage.mdx:31` → board `mode-sonnet` → vendor `cannonkeys`; `divinikey-dcs-dolch-group-buy.mdx:62` → same board/vendor; `gmk-cyl-masterpiece-r2-group-buy.mdx:83` → keycap-sets `gmk-cyl-ramune`/`gmk-cyl-selene` → vendors `kbdfans`/`prototypist`). Each article's own disclosure sentence is contradicted by markup a few lines away in the same document. `apps/web/src/components/about/AboutBody.tsx:117-124` already states the accurate version — "the 'view at vendor' button on group-buy cards, and named-part mentions that resolve to a vendor page" — making the /about page the correct source of truth and these 3 articles the outliers. (6 further articles — `cannonkeys-nyawice-group-buy.mdx`, `gmk-cyl-ishtar-r2-group-buy.mdx`, `gmk-cyl-ramune-group-buy.mdx`, `gmk-cyl-king-of-the-seas-group-buy.mdx`, `gmk-cyl-pandemonium-group-buy.mdx`, `gmk-cyl-greg-2-group-buy.mdx` — carry the same sentence with no `<PartReference>` present, so narrowly worded but not self-contradicted; out of scope for this fix)
- ease: 9 (pure text edit in 3 MDX files, no schema/component/test dependency)
- score: 5.4 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: #555
> **Resolved (2026-07-21):** reworded the "note on links"/"Vendor link note" sentence in all 3 affected articles to include the named-part-mention case, mirroring `AboutBody.tsx`'s accurate framing. Regenerated data-runtime + search manifests. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, unit, data:validate, build, 1064/1064 e2e.

### [x] [content] [3.0] Prototypist W29/W30 trend-signal spark fabricates a negative dip that never happened — addressed in this commit, closes #557
- category: content
- filed: 2026-07-21 by cloud /iterate audit (discovered via /expand pass 208 disjoint sweep, dispatched per iterate §6 failure-mode-6 when the standing audit queue had no actionable rows)
- impact: 6 (`data/trends/2026-W29.json` and `2026-W30.json`'s Prototypist rows carried a `spark` array — `[-40,-35,-30,-25,-18,-10,2,15]` then `[-35,-30,-25,-18,-10,2,15,20]` — that broke the mechanical shift-and-append convention every other week W19-W28 in the dataset followed, fabricating a negative-territory dip the vendor's real per-week `score` values (14-33 across W19-W30, never negative) never had. W29's `direction` ("up") and `note` ("...reversing last week's decline") also contradicted the real score drop 25→15 (W28→W29). The published article `prototypist-vendor-spotlight.mdx` built a whole section on the fabricated number as literal fact ("a vendor signal of -35 eight weeks before this week's reading"), the newsletter issue 04 repeated it ("Prototypist climbed from -35 to 20 across eight weeks of coverage"), and the article's InlineViz chart (`score-trajectory.svg`) visually plotted the fake trajectory — three separate published, indexed surfaces carrying the same fabrication.)
- ease: 5 (five files: two data-row edits restoring the established shift-and-append convention plus a direction/note fix, one article-prose rewrite, one newsletter-sentence rewrite, one hand-authored SVG + provenance JSON regeneration — mechanical once the root cause was traced, but multi-file)
- score: 3.0 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: #557
> **Resolved (2026-07-21):** `data/trends/2026-W29.json` Prototypist row: `direction` "up"→"down", `note` "...reversing last week's decline"→"...continuing last week's pullback", `spark` restored to `[18,20,29,27,33,28,25,15]` (shift-and-append from the real W22-W29 scores). `data/trends/2026-W30.json` Prototypist row: `spark` restored to `[20,29,27,33,28,25,15,20]`. Article's "2026 push" paragraph rewritten to the real trajectory (peaked at 33 in W26, cooled three straight weeks to 15 in W29, ticked back to 20 this week); InlineViz alt/caption updated to match. Newsletter sentence rewritten to the same real trajectory. `score-trajectory.svg` regenerated with recalculated plot coordinates for the corrected spark, updated title/caption/endpoint labels (start 24, peak 33, end +20); provenance JSON's `brief_summary`/`data_sources` updated and a `warnings`/`corrected_at` trail added documenting the fabrication and the fix. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, 673 web unit tests, data:validate (74 records), build, size (109.1 KB/200 KB budget), 1064/1064 e2e.
> Picked as the top signal this tick: no unlabeled GitHub issues (triage gate); not Monday (weekly snapshot gate n/a); no pending phases/data/content-gap work (all 7 mechanical surveys clean); march's own expand Step 3c gate not due (5 commits/~4.4h since pass 207, well under the 20-commit/48h threshold). ### [x] [a11y] [4.0] compare page Compare buttons + result links missing focus-visible ring — addressed in 575e78e, closes #562
- category: a11y
- filed: 2026-07-21 by cloud /iterate audit (deferred candidate noted in pass shipping dd2d1ef, independently re-verified this tick)
- impact: 8 (`/compare/switch` and `/compare/board` are the site's only dedicated comparison tool routes. 6 interactive elements across 4 files — the "Compare" `<button>` in `BoardCompareSelector.tsx`/`SwitchCompareSelector.tsx`, and the two result `<Link>`s in `BoardCompareTable.tsx`/`SwitchCompareTable.tsx` — carried no `focus:`/`focus-visible:` class at all, falling back to inconsistent browser-default outlines. The 4 `<select>` elements in the same components already correctly carried a visible focus ring, making the gap an inconsistency within the same two-component flow, not just a general a11y nice-to-have; keyboard-only users tabbing through the compare flow lose visible focus indication at the exact step (submit / follow-through) that matters most)
- ease: 9 (className-only edit on 6 elements, matching an existing sitewide button pattern already used in `quiz/QuizStep.tsx`; no schema/component-contract/test change)
- score: 4.0 (impact × ease / 10, using the impact value independently reassessed this tick — the deferring tick's prose estimate wasn't a formal row)
- issue: #562

Added the button pattern to both "Compare" buttons; added the same ring plus `rounded-sm` (for a clean inline-text ring) to both pairs of result links. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, 673 web unit tests (+161 script tests), data:validate, build, size (109.1 KB/200 KB budget), 1064/1064 e2e.

Two sibling articles about the identical board confirm these are different mount families in the site's own vocabulary: `mode-sonnet-r2-group-buy-coverage.mdx` calls the Sonnet's mount "gasket-mount" twice and explicitly contrasts it against a *different* board's "leaf-spring F1" mount. A reader who cross-references either sibling article — both linked from this one — hits a direct factual contradiction about the same board's core mechanical spec)

### [x] [bug] [5.4] group-buys with catalog matches never link to their /part page — addressed in this commit, closes #569
- category: bug
- filed: 2026-07-22 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep)

But all 14 records under `data/group-buys/*.json` had `productSlug: null`, and `GroupBuyRow.tsx` (rendered on `/group-buys`, `/group-buys/past`, and the vendor-page group-buy section) never read the field at all — even once populated, nothing would have changed on the page. 3 of the 14 records have an unambiguous 1:1 catalog match by name + vendor + dates: `cannonkeys-mode-sonnet-r2.json` ↔ `data/boards/mode-sonnet.json`, `kbdfans-gmk-cyl-ramune.json` ↔ `data/keycap-sets/gmk-cyl-ramune.json`, `kbdfans-gmk-cyl-selene.json` ↔ `data/keycap-sets/gmk-cyl-selene.json`. The existing `GroupBuyRow.test.tsx` fixture already defaulted to `productSlug: 'mode-sonnet'` on a "Mode Sonnet R2" fixture, confirming this exact linkage was the intended design — just never populated in real data or rendered by the component. A reader on `/group-buys/past` sees "GMK CYL Ramune" or "Mode Sonnet R2" with zero path to the rich catalog spec page for that identical product)
- ease: 9 (populate 3 JSON fields; add one conditional `<Link>` in `GroupBuyRow.tsx` reusing the existing `productKind`/`productSlug` fields already on the `GroupBuy` type; 2 new colocated unit tests + 2 new e2e assertions, no schema/component contract change)

- issue: #569

New unit tests in `GroupBuyRow.test.tsx` (link present with correct href, absent when `productSlug` is null); new e2e assertions in `group-buys.spec.ts` (`/group-buys/past` renders the link, and its href resolves to a real 200 part page). `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, 675 web unit tests (2 new), data:validate (74 records, cross-refs resolve), build, 1069/1069 e2e.

- filed: 2026-07-22 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep)
- impact: 6 (`/search` is a header-linked, sitewide tool reachable from every page — not a rarely-visited route. ### [x] [engineering] [6.5] next.js + transitive dependency vulnerabilities (SSRF/DoS in Server Actions, sharp libvips CVEs, brace-expansion regex DoS) — addressed in 316d937d, closes #587
- category: engineering
- filed: 2026-07-23 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep, dependency angle)
- impact: 7 (`pnpm audit --audit-level=high` reported 6 high advisories against `next@15.5.18` — SSRF in Server Actions/rewrites, DoS via Server Actions and Image Optimization SVGs, cache-confusion of response bodies, unauthenticated Server Function endpoint disclosure — real CVEs in the production web framework itself, sitewide by nature. Plus transitive high advisories in `sharp` (libvips CVE-2026-33327/33328/35590/35591, pulled in as next's optional image-optimization dependency) and `brace-expansion` (regex DoS, two separate vulnerable version ranges reachable via different eslint dependency chains — dev-only but still flagged high).)
- ease: 9 (patch-level `next` bump satisfied by the repo's own existing `^15.0.3`/`^15.0.0` semver ranges — no major bump, no breaking change. `sharp` and `brace-expansion` fixed via `pnpm.overrides`, the same mechanism used in the prior dependency-vuln fix `7a3f711`.)

- issue: #587

Added `pnpm.overrides` entries for `sharp@<0.35.0` and both vulnerable `brace-expansion` ranges (`>=3.0.0 <5.0.7` and `<1.1.16`, two separate dependency chains). `pnpm audit --audit-level=high` now reports zero vulnerabilities (was 6 high + 5 moderate against `next` alone, plus 2 more high against `sharp`/`brace-expansion`). `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, 684 web unit tests, 161 script tests, data:validate (74 records), build, size (108.7 KB/200 KB budget), 1074/1074 e2e.

### [x] [perf] [4.0] no ISR revalidation anywhere in the app — date-sensitive group-buy pages frozen at build/deploy time — addressed in 31f16355, closes #590
- category: perf
- filed: 2026-07-23 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep — angles disjoint from recent ticks: ISR/revalidate coverage, canonical URL/trailing-slash handling, meta description length/duplication, per-page robots meta, sitemap lastmod accuracy, favicon/manifest routes, group-buy date-math edge cases, next.config.js image domains, security headers, print stylesheet, tag/vendor empty states, JSON-LD field completeness, empty catch blocks, duplicate tags, frontmatter date sanity, OG route path construction)
- impact: 5 (`grep -rn revalidate apps/web/src` returned zero matches — every route was fully static once built, with no periodic regeneration. `app/page.tsx` (home widget), `app/group-buys/page.tsx`, `app/group-buys/past/page.tsx`, and `app/vendor/[slug]/page.tsx` all compute `new Date()` at render time to bucket group buys into live/announced/ended and drive "closing soon" urgency framing, but that date math was frozen at build/deploy time rather than recomputed per request — contradicting `spec.md:134`'s stated architecture, "ISR for freshness". A buy whose `endDate` had passed could keep rendering as live with a moving-looking progress bar, or an announced buy past its `startDate` could stay stuck as "Announced", until the next push regenerated the page. These are the site's most commercially time-sensitive surfaces.)
- ease: 8 (one-line `export const revalidate = 3600` added to each of the 4 affected page files; no logic changes, no test rewrites)
- score: 4.0 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: #590
> **Resolved (2026-07-23):** Added `export const revalidate = 3600` to `app/page.tsx`, `app/group-buys/page.tsx`, `app/group-buys/past/page.tsx`, and `app/vendor/[slug]/page.tsx`. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, 689 web unit tests, data:validate (74 records), build, 1074/1074 e2e.

### [x] [engineering] [4.8] tracker-linkage-survey.mjs topic matching breaks run continuity on trend-row name spelling drift — addressed in f811b20b
- category: engineering
- filed: 2026-07-24 by cloud /iterate audit (follow-up on the e2cd04fc tick's own sweep, which flagged this as a real but deferred gap)
- impact: 6 (`findMissingLinks` in `scripts/tracker-linkage-survey.mjs` — the mechanical enforcer of bearings.md Rule 2 — keyed its per-topic run-continuity map on the raw `row.name` string. `data/trends/*.json` names drift week to week: case ("75% layout" vs "75% Layout"), slash spacing ("Hall Effect / Rapid Trigger" vs "Hall Effect/Rapid Trigger", confirmed at the real W28→W29 boundary), and other phrasing variants — 7+ distinct drift pairs found across the 12 snapshot files. Each drift point silently reset the tracked run-start date to the later spelling, exactly the "run continuity" failure mode this same script's docstring already documents fixing twice before (the "ever linked" masking across W22, W24/W26, W28) — just triggered by spelling instead of a flat/absent gap. Currently latent (every real drift point happens to carry an `articleSlug` on both sides today), but a future drift on an unlinked topic would understate its true age and delay the Rule-2 14-day flag)
- ease: 8 (isolated to one function in one script; add a `normalizeTopicKey()` helper for the map key while keeping the latest raw string for display; existing `node:test` harness already covers this exact scenario class)
- score: 4.8 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: (none filed this tick — the fix shipped directly from the audit sweep before the mirror step was invoked, same as the precedent set by the 864cfe8-class ticks)
> **Resolved (2026-07-24):** added `normalizeTopicKey()` (case-fold, collapse whitespace, normalize " / "/"/" slash spacing) as the `topicMap` key in `findMissingLinks`, with `entry.displayName` tracking the most-recently-seen raw string for row output. 7 new regression tests in `scripts/__tests__/tracker-linkage-survey.test.mjs` reproduce the real Hall-Effect/Rapid-Trigger and 75%-layout drifts, plus direct `normalizeTopicKey()` unit coverage. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, 168 script tests (+18), web unit tests, data:validate (74 records), build, size (108.7 KB/200 KB budget), 1074/1074 e2e.

### [x] [bug] [5.4] tracker summary riser/faller can mislabel a flat row as moving — addressed in d63e2890, closes #599
- category: bug
- filed: 2026-07-24 by cloud /iterate audit (fresh general-purpose sweep)
- impact: 6 (`pickRiser`/`pickFaller` in `apps/web/src/lib/tracker/index.ts` fell back to any remaining row — including `direction: 'flat'` — when no `up`/`down` rows were left in a snapshot, the identical failure mode already found and fixed once for `pickSleeper` per `plan/CRITIQUE.md` pass 9 #8. A week with zero up rows (or zero down rows after riser is picked) would render a "BIGGEST RISER"/"BIGGEST FALLER" card with a self-contradictory "flat" delta and flat-tone glyph, and the same-day-shipped `describeTrackerWeek` SEO/JSON-LD copy would name a flat row as the week's "Top mover". Not live in any of the 12 current `data/trends/*.json` snapshots today, but W27 already had 8 of 19 rows flat — the trend is drifting toward triggering this)
- ease: 9 (mirror the existing `pickSleeper` fix pattern in the same file; no schema/component change)
- score: 5.4 (impact × ease / 10)
- issue: #599
> **Resolved (2026-07-24):** `pickRiser`/`pickFaller` now only draw from their respective `up`/`down` direction and drop the slot (return `null`) instead of falling back to flat rows. Added regression tests for zero-up, zero-down, and all-flat snapshots plus a `describeTrackerWeek` test asserting no flat "Top mover" clause is ever emitted; updated one pre-existing `helpers.test.ts` test that had locked in the old buggy fallback behavior. `pnpm verify` full gate green: typecheck, 700 web unit tests (+4), 168 script tests, data:validate (74 records), build, size (108.7 KB/200 KB budget), 1074/1074 e2e.
